# Airo TV Unified Browse Experience Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** One responsive browse experience (phone/tablet/TV/desktop) rendered from generated rails, with renderer-agnostic player overlay and staged playlist import, per `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-19-airo-tv-unified-browse-design.md`.

**Architecture:** Rails are generated by a `RailProvider` abstraction in the Media Engine layer (`platform_channels` package — it already owns `IPTVChannel` and `channel_data_service`); Flutter renders whatever rail list arrives. One `MediaCard` widget with variants replaces per-content-type cards. Player overlay consumes only published `PlayerState`. Import becomes a staged pipeline with a progress stream.

**Tech Stack:** Flutter, Riverpod, Equatable. Existing widgets reused: `AiroRail`, `AiroRailCard`, `TvFocusable`, `AiroResponsiveScaffold`, `AdaptiveNavigation`.

## Global Constraints

- Design tokens per spec §2 — accent green `oklch(0.62 0.19 145)` ≈ `Color(0xFF34C97B)`, live red ≈ `Color(0xFFD84A3A)`, gold ≈ `Color(0xFFE0B15C)`, bg ≈ `Color(0xFF101216)`. Read colors through `Theme.of(context)` / `AiroThemeTokens`, never hardcode in widgets.
- Navigation destinations everywhere, exact order: **Home, Live, Guide, Favorites, Settings**.
- Phone (≤440 logical px): 5-item bottom `NavigationBar`. TV/desktop: left rail (existing `tv_shell.dart`).
- Player overlay must not import ExoPlayer/VLC/engine types — only `platform_player` published models.
- Rails never hardcoded in UI. UI renders `List<RailResult>` from a Riverpod provider.
- All new public widgets/classes get dartdoc. `flutter analyze` clean after every task.
- Run tests with `flutter test <path>` from the owning package directory.
- Known pre-existing failure: none expected; if `riverpod_lint`/`custom_lint` analyzer_plugin conflict blocks `flutter analyze` (observation 8116), use `dart analyze` instead.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```dart
// packages/platform_channels/test/models/rail_definition_test.dart
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
import 'package:platform_channels/platform_channels.dart';

void main() {
  group('RailQuery.matches', () {
    const sports = IPTVChannel(
      id: '1',
      name: 'Star Sports 1 HD',
      streamUrl: 'http://x/1.m3u8',
      category: ChannelCategory.sports,
      languages: ['hi', 'en'],
    );
    const news = IPTVChannel(
      id: '2',
      name: 'Aaj Tak HD',
      streamUrl: 'http://x/2.m3u8',
      category: ChannelCategory.news,
      languages: ['hi'],
    );

    test('category filter matches only that category', () {
      const q = RailQuery(category: ChannelCategory.sports);
      expect(q.matches(sports), isTrue);
      expect(q.matches(news), isFalse);
    });

    test('language filter matches any listed language', () {
      const q = RailQuery(language: 'hi');
      expect(q.matches(sports), isTrue);
      expect(q.matches(news), isTrue);
      const en = RailQuery(language: 'ta');
      expect(en.matches(news), isFalse);
    });

    test('empty query matches everything', () {
      const q = RailQuery();
      expect(q.matches(sports), isTrue);
      expect(q.matches(news), isTrue);
    });
  });

  test('RailDefinition equality is value-based', () {
    const a = RailDefinition(
      id: 'live-sports',
      title: 'Live Sports',
      query: RailQuery(category: ChannelCategory.sports),
      priority: 10,
    );
    const b = RailDefinition(
      id: 'live-sports',
      title: 'Live Sports',
      query: RailQuery(category: ChannelCategory.sports),
      priority: 10,
    );
    expect(a, equals(b));
  });
}
```

Note: check `ChannelCategory` enum values in `packages/platform_channels/lib/src/models/iptv_channel.dart` before writing — use real member names (e.g. if it's `ChannelCategory.sport` not `.sports`, adjust the test).

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `cd packages/platform_channels && flutter test test/models/rail_definition_test.dart`
Expected: FAIL — `RailQuery` undefined.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```dart
// packages/platform_channels/lib/src/models/rail_definition.dart
import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';

import 'iptv_channel.dart';

/// When a rail is shown in the browse screen.
enum RailVisibility { always, whenNonEmpty, hidden }

/// Card presentation for a rail's channels.
enum RailLayout { standard, hero, compact }

/// Declarative filter for selecting a rail's channels.
///
/// All non-null fields must match (AND semantics). An empty query
/// matches every channel.
class RailQuery extends Equatable {
  const RailQuery({
    this.category,
    this.language,
    this.liveOnly = false,
    this.favoritesOnly = false,
  });

  final ChannelCategory? category;
  final String? language;
  final bool liveOnly;
  final bool favoritesOnly;

  bool matches(IPTVChannel channel) {
    if (category != null && channel.category != category) return false;
    if (language != null && !channel.languages.contains(language)) {
      return false;
    }
    return true;
  }

  @override
  List<Object?> get props => [category, language, liveOnly, favoritesOnly];
}

/// A generated rail: what it's called, what it contains, where it sits.
///
/// The intelligence layer can add, remove, and reorder definitions without
/// any Flutter change — the UI renders whatever list it receives.
class RailDefinition extends Equatable {
  const RailDefinition({
    required this.id,
    required this.title,
    required this.query,
    required this.priority,
    this.subtitle,
    this.visibility = RailVisibility.whenNonEmpty,
    this.layout = RailLayout.standard,
    this.maxItems = 20,
  });

  final String id;
  final String title;
  final String? subtitle;
  final RailQuery query;

  /// Lower renders first.
  final int priority;
  final RailVisibility visibility;
  final RailLayout layout;
  final int maxItems;

  @override
  List<Object?> get props =>
      [id, title, subtitle, query, priority, visibility, layout, maxItems];
}

/// A built rail ready for rendering.
class RailResult extends Equatable {
  const RailResult({required this.definition, required this.channels});

  final RailDefinition definition;
  final List<IPTVChannel> channels;

  @override
  List<Object?> get props => [definition, channels];
}

/// Builds the channel list for a rail definition.
///
/// Media Engine implements this from playlist metadata, EPG, watch history,
/// and provider data; the Edge Intelligence SDK later personalizes and
/// reorders. The UI never implements this.
// ignore: one_member_abstracts
abstract class RailProvider {
  Future<List<IPTVChannel>> buildRail(RailDefinition rail);
}
```

### Task 2: DefaultRailProvider with popularity scoring

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/platform_channels/lib/src/services/default_rail_provider.dart`
- Modify: `packages/platform_channels/lib/platform_channels.dart` (add export)
- Test: `packages/platform_channels/test/services/default_rail_provider_test.dart`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `RailDefinition`, `RailQuery`, `RailProvider`, `RailResult` (Task 1).
- Produces:
  - `class DefaultRailProvider implements RailProvider` with constructor `DefaultRailProvider({required List<IPTVChannel> channels, Set<String> favoriteIds = const {}, Map<String, int> watchCounts = const {}})`
  - `Future<List<IPTVChannel>> buildRail(RailDefinition rail)`
  - `Future<List<RailResult>> buildAll(List<RailDefinition> definitions)` — sorted by priority, `whenNonEmpty` empties dropped
  - `class DefaultRailCatalog` with `static List<RailDefinition> definitions()` returning the v1 set: `top-india`, `live-sports`, `movies-on-now`, `hindi-news`, `favorites`, `recently-added`.
- Consumed by Task 3.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```dart
// packages/platform_channels/test/services/default_rail_provider_test.dart
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
import 'package:platform_channels/platform_channels.dart';

void main() {
  const a = IPTVChannel(id: 'a', name: 'A', streamUrl: 'u');
  const b = IPTVChannel(id: 'b', name: 'B', streamUrl: 'u');
  const c = IPTVChannel(id: 'c', name: 'C', streamUrl: 'u');

  group('popularity ordering', () {
    test('favorites outrank watch history, which outranks provider order',
        () async {
      final provider = DefaultRailProvider(
        channels: const [a, b, c],
        favoriteIds: const {'c'},
        watchCounts: const {'b': 3},
      );
      final rail = await provider.buildRail(const RailDefinition(
        id: 'top', title: 'Top', query: RailQuery(), priority: 0,
      ));
      expect(rail.map((ch) => ch.id).toList(), ['c', 'b', 'a']);
    });

    test('falls back to provider order when no signals', () async {
      final provider = DefaultRailProvider(channels: const [a, b, c]);
      final rail = await provider.buildRail(const RailDefinition(
        id: 'top', title: 'Top', query: RailQuery(), priority: 0,
      ));
      expect(rail.map((ch) => ch.id).toList(), ['a', 'b', 'c']);
    });
  });

  group('buildAll', () {
    test('sorts by priority and drops empty whenNonEmpty rails', () async {
      final provider = DefaultRailProvider(channels: const [a]);
      final results = await provider.buildAll(const [
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'second', title: 'Second', query: RailQuery(), priority: 20,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'first', title: 'First', query: RailQuery(), priority: 10,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'empty',
          title: 'Empty',
          // favoritesOnly with no favorites → empty → dropped
          query: RailQuery(favoritesOnly: true),
          priority: 0,
        ),
      ]);
      expect(results.map((r) => r.definition.id).toList(),
          ['first', 'second']);
    });
  });

  test('DefaultRailCatalog exposes the v1 rail set', () {
    final ids = DefaultRailCatalog.definitions().map((d) => d.id).toList();
    expect(
      ids,
      containsAll(<String>[
        'top-india', 'live-sports', 'movies-on-now',
        'hindi-news', 'favorites', 'recently-added',
      ]),
    );
  });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `cd packages/platform_channels && flutter test test/services/default_rail_provider_test.dart`
Expected: FAIL — `DefaultRailProvider` undefined.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write implementation**

```dart
// packages/platform_channels/lib/src/services/default_rail_provider.dart
import '../models/iptv_channel.dart';
import '../models/rail_definition.dart';

/// v1 rail set. The intelligence layer will later supply definitions;
/// this catalog is the Media Engine default.
class DefaultRailCatalog {
  const DefaultRailCatalog._();

  static List<RailDefinition> definitions() => const [
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'top-india',
          title: 'Top India',
          subtitle: 'Ranked by popularity',
          query: RailQuery(),
          priority: 0,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'favorites',
          title: 'Favorites',
          subtitle: 'Your saved channels',
          query: RailQuery(favoritesOnly: true),
          priority: 10,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'live-sports',
          title: 'Live Sports',
          subtitle: 'Cricket, football and more',
          query: RailQuery(category: ChannelCategory.sports, liveOnly: true),
          priority: 20,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'hindi-news',
          title: 'Hindi News',
          subtitle: 'Breaking news and analysis',
          query: RailQuery(category: ChannelCategory.news, language: 'hi'),
          priority: 30,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'movies-on-now',
          title: 'Movies On Now',
          subtitle: 'Playing right now',
          query: RailQuery(category: ChannelCategory.movies),
          priority: 40,
        ),
        RailDefinition(
          id: 'recently-added',
          title: 'Recently Added',
          query: RailQuery(),
          priority: 50,
        ),
      ];
}

/// Popularity-scored rail builder: favorites + watch history + provider
/// order, degrading to provider order alone when signals are absent.
/// Later replaced by regional popularity / trending / AI ranking behind
/// the same [RailProvider] interface — zero UI change.
class DefaultRailProvider implements RailProvider {
  DefaultRailProvider({
    required List<IPTVChannel> channels,
    this.favoriteIds = const {},
    this.watchCounts = const {},
  }) : _channels = channels;

  final List<IPTVChannel> _channels;
  final Set<String> favoriteIds;
  final Map<String, int> watchCounts;

  static const _favoriteWeight = 1000000;
  static const _watchWeight = 1000;

  int _score(IPTVChannel ch, int providerIndex) {
    final favorite = favoriteIds.contains(ch.id) ? _favoriteWeight : 0;
    final watched = (watchCounts[ch.id] ?? 0) * _watchWeight;
    // Provider order: earlier = higher. Bounded below _watchWeight so one
    // watch always outranks any provider position.
    final order = (_channels.length - providerIndex).clamp(0, _watchWeight - 1);
    return favorite + watched + order;
  }

  @override
  Future<List<IPTVChannel>> buildRail(RailDefinition rail) async {
    Iterable<IPTVChannel> pool = _channels.where(rail.query.matches);
    if (rail.query.favoritesOnly) {
      pool = pool.where((ch) => favoriteIds.contains(ch.id));
    }
    // liveOnly: IPTV live channels are the default content type; VOD
    // filtering is applied upstream. Kept as a declared intent for the
    // intelligence layer.
    final indexed = pool.toList();
    final providerIndex = <String, int>{
      for (var i = 0; i < _channels.length; i++) _channels[i].id: i,
    };
    indexed.sort((x, y) => _score(y, providerIndex[y.id] ?? 0)
        .compareTo(_score(x, providerIndex[x.id] ?? 0)));
    return indexed.take(rail.maxItems).toList();
  }

  /// Builds every rail, sorted by [RailDefinition.priority]; rails with
  /// [RailVisibility.whenNonEmpty] and no channels are dropped, and
  /// [RailVisibility.hidden] rails are skipped entirely.
  Future<List<RailResult>> buildAll(List<RailDefinition> definitions) async {
    final sorted = [...definitions]
      ..sort((x, y) => x.priority.compareTo(y.priority));
    final results = <RailResult>[];
    for (final def in sorted) {
      if (def.visibility == RailVisibility.hidden) continue;
      final channels = await buildRail(def);
      if (channels.isEmpty && def.visibility == RailVisibility.whenNonEmpty) {
        continue;
      }
      results.add(RailResult(definition: def, channels: channels));
    }
    return results;
  }
}
```

Adjust `ChannelCategory.sports` / `.news` / `.movies` to the real enum member names found in Task 1.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement**

```dart
// packages/feature_iptv/lib/application/providers/rails_provider.dart
import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
import 'package:platform_channels/platform_channels.dart';

import 'iptv_providers.dart';

/// The generated browse rails. UI renders this list verbatim — rail
/// content, ordering, and visibility are decided by the Media Engine
/// (and later the Edge Intelligence SDK), never by widgets.
final railsProvider = FutureProvider<List<RailResult>>((ref) async {
  final channels = await ref.watch(/* main channel list provider */.future);
  final favorites =
      await ref.watch(favoriteChannelsProvider.future).catchError(
            (Object _) => <IPTVChannel>[],
          );
  final provider = DefaultRailProvider(
    channels: channels,
    favoriteIds: favorites.map((ch) => ch.id).toSet(),
    // Watch counts arrive when core_watch_progress wiring lands
    // (spec §9 — Continue Watching reserved).
  );
  return provider.buildAll(DefaultRailCatalog.definitions());
});
```

Export `rails_provider.dart` from `packages/feature_iptv/lib/feature_iptv.dart` if application providers are re-exported there (check the barrel; follow existing pattern for `iptv_providers.dart`).

### Task 4: MediaCard with variants in core_ui

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/core_ui/lib/src/widgets/media_card.dart`
- Modify: `packages/core_ui/lib/core_ui.dart` (or existing widget barrel — follow `airo_rail_card.dart`'s export path)
- Test: `packages/core_ui/test/widgets/media_card_test.dart`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `AiroRailCard` (existing, `packages/core_ui/lib/src/widgets/airo_rail_card.dart`), `TvFocusable`, `AiroThemeTokens`.
- Produces:
  - `enum MediaCardVariant { compact, standard, hero, live }` — spec §4.2 lists more variants (`landscape`, `portrait`, `continueWatching`); those are added to the enum when first used (YAGNI). `standard` IS the 172×104 landscape card.
  - `class MediaCard extends StatelessWidget` with constructor `MediaCard({required String name, MediaCardVariant variant = MediaCardVariant.standard, String? subtitle, String? logoUrl, String? initials, Color? placeholderColor, String? quality, bool isLive = false, VoidCallback? onTap, VoidCallback? onLongPress, bool autofocus = false})`
- Consumed by Tasks 7, 8.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```dart
// packages/core_ui/test/widgets/media_card_test.dart
import 'package:core_ui/core_ui.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';

Widget _host(Widget child) => MaterialApp(home: Scaffold(body: child));

void main() {
  testWidgets('standard variant renders name, subtitle, quality badge',
      (tester) async {
    await tester.pumpWidget(_host(const MediaCard(
      name: 'Star Sports 1 HD',
      subtitle: 'IND vs AUS',
      quality: 'HD',
    )));
    expect(find.text('Star Sports 1 HD'), findsOneWidget);
    expect(find.text('IND vs AUS'), findsOneWidget);
    expect(find.text('HD'), findsOneWidget);
  });

  testWidgets('live variant shows LIVE badge', (tester) async {
    await tester.pumpWidget(_host(const MediaCard(
      name: 'Aaj Tak',
      variant: MediaCardVariant.live,
    )));
    expect(find.text('LIVE'), findsOneWidget);
  });

  testWidgets('hero variant is wider than standard', (tester) async {
    await tester.pumpWidget(_host(const Row(children: [
      MediaCard(name: 'A', variant: MediaCardVariant.hero),
    ])));
    final size = tester.getSize(find.byType(MediaCard));
    expect(size.width, greaterThan(172));
  });

  testWidgets('compact variant is narrower than standard', (tester) async {
    await tester.pumpWidget(_host(const Row(children: [
      MediaCard(name: 'A', variant: MediaCardVariant.compact),
    ])));
    final size = tester.getSize(find.byType(MediaCard));
    expect(size.width, lessThan(172));
  });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `cd packages/core_ui && flutter test test/widgets/media_card_test.dart`
Expected: FAIL — `MediaCard` undefined.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement**

```dart
// packages/core_ui/lib/src/widgets/media_card.dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

import 'airo_rail_card.dart';

/// Presentation variants for [MediaCard].
///
/// One card component serves every content type (spec §4.2): variant
/// controls size, aspect, and badge set — never a separate widget per
/// content type. Additional variants (portrait, continueWatching) are
/// added here when the first call site needs them.
enum MediaCardVariant {
  /// Small rail card (140×84 thumbnail area).
  compact,

  /// The default 172×104 landscape rail card from the source design.
  standard,

  /// Large featured banner card (320×180 thumbnail area).
  hero,

  /// Standard size with the pulsing LIVE badge forced on.
  live,
}

/// The single reusable media card: channel, movie, show — one widget.
///
/// Wraps [AiroRailCard] (which owns the visual treatment: focus scaling,
/// badges, initials fallback) and maps [variant] to concrete dimensions.
class MediaCard extends StatelessWidget {
  const MediaCard({
    required this.name,
    super.key,
    this.variant = MediaCardVariant.standard,
    this.subtitle,
    this.logoUrl,
    this.initials,
    this.placeholderColor,
    this.quality,
    this.isLive = false,
    this.onTap,
    this.onLongPress,
    this.autofocus = false,
  });

  final String name;
  final MediaCardVariant variant;
  final String? subtitle;
  final String? logoUrl;
  final String? initials;
  final Color? placeholderColor;
  final String? quality;
  final bool isLive;
  final VoidCallback? onTap;
  final VoidCallback? onLongPress;
  final bool autofocus;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final (width, thumbnailHeight) = switch (variant) {
      MediaCardVariant.compact => (140.0, 84.0),
      MediaCardVariant.standard || MediaCardVariant.live => (172.0, 104.0),
      MediaCardVariant.hero => (320.0, 180.0),
    };
    return AiroRailCard(
      name: name,
      subtitle: subtitle,
      logoUrl: logoUrl,
      initials: initials,
      placeholderColor: placeholderColor,
      quality: quality,
      isLive: isLive || variant == MediaCardVariant.live,
      width: width,
      thumbnailHeight: thumbnailHeight,
      onTap: onTap,
      onLongPress: onLongPress,
      autofocus: autofocus,
    );
  }
}
```

### Task 6: Chunk-1/2 boundary — mobile BrowseScreen with rails

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/feature_iptv/lib/presentation/screens/browse_screen.dart`
- Modify: `packages/feature_iptv/lib/presentation/screens/iptv_screen.dart` (mobile body swap)
- Test: `packages/feature_iptv/test/presentation/browse_screen_test.dart`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `railsProvider` (Task 3), `MediaCard` (Task 4), `AiroRail` (existing core_ui), category providers from `iptv_providers.dart` (the existing category filter state used by `_PrimaryCategoryBar` at `iptv_screen.dart:948`).
- Produces: `class BrowseScreen extends ConsumerWidget` — the single browse body used by phone. Constructor: `BrowseScreen({void Function(IPTVChannel)? onChannelSelected})`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing widget test**

```dart
// packages/feature_iptv/test/presentation/browse_screen_test.dart
import 'package:feature_iptv/feature_iptv.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
import 'package:platform_channels/platform_channels.dart';

void main() {
  testWidgets('renders one AiroRail per RailResult with MediaCards',
      (tester) async {
    const ch = IPTVChannel(id: 'x', name: 'Star Sports', streamUrl: 'u');
    final rails = [
      const RailResult(
        definition: RailDefinition(
          id: 'top-india', title: 'Top India', query: RailQuery(), priority: 0,
        ),
        channels: [ch],
      ),
    ];
    await tester.pumpWidget(ProviderScope(
      overrides: [railsProvider.overrideWith((ref) async => rails)],
      child: const MaterialApp(home: BrowseScreen()),
    ));
    await tester.pumpAndSettle();
    expect(find.text('Top India'), findsOneWidget);
    expect(find.text('Star Sports'), findsOneWidget);
  });

  testWidgets('shows loading indicator while rails build', (tester) async {
    await tester.pumpWidget(ProviderScope(
      overrides: [
        railsProvider.overrideWith((ref) => Future.any([])),
      ],
      child: const MaterialApp(home: BrowseScreen()),
    ));
    await tester.pump();
    expect(find.byType(CircularProgressIndicator), findsOneWidget);
  });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run — FAIL.** `cd packages/feature_iptv && flutter test test/presentation/browse_screen_test.dart`

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement BrowseScreen**

```dart
// packages/feature_iptv/lib/presentation/screens/browse_screen.dart
import 'package:core_ui/core_ui.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
import 'package:platform_channels/platform_channels.dart';

import '../../application/providers/rails_provider.dart';

/// The unified browse body (spec §5.2): rail list generated by the Media
/// Engine, rendered identically on phone/tablet/TV/desktop. Only spacing,
/// card sizes, and focus behavior adapt per form factor.
class BrowseScreen extends ConsumerWidget {
  const BrowseScreen({super.key, this.onChannelSelected});

  /// Invoked when a card is tapped/selected. The host screen decides
  /// whether that means "play inline", "open player route", etc.
  final void Function(IPTVChannel channel)? onChannelSelected;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) {
    final rails = ref.watch(railsProvider);
    return rails.when(
      loading: () => const Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator()),
      error: (error, _) => Center(
        child: Text('Could not build rails: $error'),
      ),
      data: (results) => ListView(
        padding: const EdgeInsets.only(top: 12, bottom: 20),
        children: [
          for (final result in results)
            AiroRail(
              title: result.definition.title,
              subtitle: result.definition.subtitle,
              children: [
                for (final channel in result.channels)
                  MediaCard(
                    name: channel.name,
                    subtitle: channel.group,
                    logoUrl: channel.logoUrl,
                    initials: channel.name.isEmpty
                        ? '?'
                        : channel.name
                            .split(' ')
                            .take(2)
                            .map((w) => w.isEmpty ? '' : w[0])
                            .join()
                            .toUpperCase(),
                    variant: result.definition.layout == RailLayout.hero
                        ? MediaCardVariant.hero
                        : MediaCardVariant.standard,
                    onTap: () => onChannelSelected?.call(channel),
                  ),
              ],
            ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

Export from `feature_iptv.dart` barrel.

- [ ] **Step 4: Wire into mobile IPTV screen.** In `iptv_screen.dart`, locate the mobile browse body (the `_StreamTabContent` region around line 522 / the category-bar + panel composition around `_PrimaryCategoryBar`:948). Replace the channel-list body for the browse tab with `BrowseScreen(onChannelSelected: <existing channel-play callback>)`, keeping the existing top bar, search, and playlist-sheet triggers. Keep the old category bar above the rails (it maps to the design's category chips; a later polish pass restyles it). Do not delete old widgets yet — the guide/other tabs still use them.

- [ ] **Step 5: Empty state (spec §5.1).** Mobile already has `_BringYourOwnPlaylistView` (`iptv_screen.dart:892`) and TV has the checklist empty state (`iptv_tv_screen.dart` `_EmptyStateChecklistItem`). Keep both as the no-playlist state; `BrowseScreen` is only mounted when channels exist. No new empty-state widget — restyle to the mockup's checkmark layout only if the mobile view diverges visibly from it (compare while dogfooding).

- [ ] **Step 6: Run tests.** `cd packages/feature_iptv && flutter test`
Expected: new tests pass; fix any of the 10 `iptv_screen_test.dart` tests that asserted the old browse body (update expectations to `BrowseScreen`).

- [ ] **Step 7: Dogfood checkpoint (end of Chunks 1+2 mobile).** Run the app (`run` skill, phone-size window or device). Verify: 5-item bottom nav, browse shows rails from the loaded playlist, tap card plays. Screenshot for review.

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit**

```bash
git add packages/feature_iptv/lib/presentation/screens/browse_screen.dart packages/feature_iptv/lib/presentation/screens/iptv_screen.dart packages/feature_iptv/lib/feature_iptv.dart packages/feature_iptv/test/presentation/browse_screen_test.dart
```

## Chunk 3 — Player

### Task 8: Renderer-agnostic PlayerState aggregate

**Files:**
- Read first: `packages/platform_player/lib/src/models/streaming_state.dart`, `playback_engine_models.dart`, `multi_source_failover_models.dart`
- Create: `packages/platform_player/lib/src/models/player_view_state.dart`
- Modify: `packages/platform_player/lib/platform_player.dart` (export)
- Test: `packages/platform_player/test/models/player_view_state_test.dart`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: existing `PlaybackState`, `NetworkQuality`, `LiveStreamState` enums; failover source index/count from `multi_source_failover_models.dart` (read for exact names).
- Produces (spec §4.3 — overlay renders ONLY this):

```dart
class PlayerViewState extends Equatable {
  const PlayerViewState({
    this.playback = PlaybackState.idle,
    this.liveState = LiveStreamState.unknown,
    this.networkQuality = NetworkQuality.good,
    this.bufferSeconds = 0,
    this.qualityLabel = 'Auto',
    this.title = '',
    this.subtitle = '',
    this.failover,
  });

  final PlaybackState playback;
  final LiveStreamState liveState;
  final NetworkQuality networkQuality;
  final int bufferSeconds;
  final String qualityLabel;
  final String title;
  final String subtitle;
  final FailoverProgress? failover;
  // + props, copyWith
}

/// Non-null while a source switch is in flight → drives the failover toast.
class FailoverProgress extends Equatable {
  const FailoverProgress({required this.currentSource, required this.totalSources});
  final int currentSource; // 1-based
  final int totalSources;
}
```

Named `PlayerViewState` (not `PlayerState`) to avoid colliding with any existing engine-side `PlayerState` — grep first; if no collision exists, `PlayerState` is fine, update Task 9 accordingly.

- [ ] **Step 1: Failing test** — value equality, `copyWith` preserves unset fields, default state is idle/Auto/no-failover.
- [ ] **Step 2: Run — FAIL.** `cd packages/platform_player && flutter test test/models/player_view_state_test.dart`
- [ ] **Step 3: Implement + export.**
- [ ] **Step 4: Run full package tests — PASS.**

```dart
class PlayerOverlay extends StatefulWidget {
  const PlayerOverlay({
    required this.state,          // PlayerViewState
    required this.onBack,         // VoidCallback
    required this.onPlayPause,    // VoidCallback
    this.onPrevious,              // VoidCallback?
    this.onNext,                  // VoidCallback?
    this.onGuide,                 // VoidCallback?

}
```

## Chunk 4 — Import

### Task 11: Staged import pipeline with progress stream

**Files:**
- Read first: `packages/platform_channels/lib/src/services/channel_data_service.dart` (existing fetch/parse/dedupe flow)
- Create: `packages/platform_channels/lib/src/models/import_progress.dart`
- Modify: `packages/platform_channels/lib/src/services/channel_data_service.dart`, barrel export
- Test: `packages/platform_channels/test/services/import_progress_test.dart`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces (spec §4.4):

```dart
enum ImportStage {
  import_, validate, download, parse, normalize,
  deduplicate, index, generateRails, persist, ready, failed,
}

class ImportProgress extends Equatable {
  const ImportProgress({
    required this.stage,
    this.fraction = 0.0,   // 0..1 within the stage
    this.message,
    this.error,
  });
}
```

  plus a method on the existing service: `Stream<ImportProgress> importPlaylist(String url)` that wraps the current fetch→parse→dedupe steps, emitting one `ImportProgress` per stage transition (stages the current code doesn't distinguish — e.g. normalize vs index — may be emitted back-to-back; the enum contract is complete even if v1 durations are near-zero). Terminal emission: `ImportStage.ready` with the channel count in `message`, or `ImportStage.failed` with `error`.
- Consumed by Task 12.

- [ ] **Step 1: Failing test** — collect emissions from `importPlaylist` against a stubbed fetch (inject or mock the HTTP layer the way existing `channel_data_service` tests do — read them first): expect stage sequence starts `[import_, validate, download, parse, ...]`, ends `ready`; a malformed-URL run ends `failed` with non-null `error` and no `ready`.
- [ ] **Step 2: Run — FAIL.** **Step 3: Implement.** Existing one-shot import API stays; `importPlaylist` becomes the primary path and the old API delegates to it (collect stream, return final result) so no caller breaks.
- [ ] **Step 4: Full package tests — PASS.**
- [ ] **Step 5: Commit** — `feat(platform_channels): staged playlist import pipeline with ImportProgress stream`

## Final verification

- [ ] `flutter analyze` (or `dart analyze`) clean in every touched package: `platform_channels`, `core_ui`, `feature_iptv`, `platform_player`, `app`.
- [ ] Full test suites green: `cd <pkg> && flutter test` for each touched package + `cd app && flutter test`.
- [ ] Dogfood matrix: phone-size (bottom nav, browse rails, player overlay, mini bar, import progress) + TV via run-airo-tv (rail band, DPAD focus, sidebar).
- [ ] Review gates (spec §7): chief-architect (Tasks 1-3 package boundaries), tv-experience-architect (Tasks 7, 9), playback-architect (Tasks 8-9 PlayerViewState contract), chief-ux-officer (Task 5 nav model).
- [ ] Theme tokens: existing `AiroThemeTokens` (gridLine/chromeSurface/glow/success/warning) already back the design's green/gold/live palette on TV; widgets in this plan read only those + `ColorScheme`. If dogfood shows a palette mismatch vs the mockup, adjust token values in the theme definition — no new token class.
- [ ] Not in this plan (spec §9 / deferred): DM Sans font bundling (design uses it, but token/typography audit showed no font infra in pubspecs — file as follow-up issue rather than blocking), hero featured banner on mobile (exists on TV at `iptv_tv_screen.dart:431`), Continue Watching rail, real ranking signals.
